data.title: Maximum Downward Path Sum
data.statement:
Compute the maximum possible sum along a path from the top row to the bottom row of a grid of integers.
The grid has [[R]] rows and [[C]] columns.
The path:

Starts in any cell of the first row
Ends in any cell of the last row
Moves down by exactly one row at a time

From position ([[r]], [[c]]), the next position can be:

([[r + 1]], [[c – 1]])
([[r + 1]], [[c]])
([[r + 1]], [[c + 1]])

Moves that go outside the grid are not allowed.
data.stubGenerator:
read R:int C:int
loop R loopline C value:int
write answer
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1:>> Two integers [[R]] and [[C]] representing the number of rows and columns.
<<Next [[R]] lines:>> [[C]] space‑separated integers representing the grid values.
data.outputDescription: <<Line 1:>> A single integer representing the maximum possible path sum.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

Compute the maximum possible sum along a path from the top row to the bottom row of a grid of integers.
The grid has R rows and C columns.
The path:

Starts in any cell of the first row
Ends in any cell of the last row
Moves down by exactly one row at a time

From position (r, c), the next position can be:

(r + 1, c – 1)
(r + 1, c)
(r + 1, c + 1)

Moves that go outside the grid are not allowed.
Input
Line 1: Two integers R and C representing the number of rows and columns.
Next R lines: C space‑separated integers representing the grid values.
Output
Line 1: A single integer representing the maximum possible path sum.
Constraints
1 ≤ R ≤ 10
1 ≤ C ≤ 10
−10 ≤ each grid value ≤ 10
Example
Input
3 3
1 2 3
4 5 6
7 8 9
Output
18
